- **Score:** 0.34
- **Source:** Federal Register
- **Published:** 2026-03-06
- **Type:** Proposed Rule
- **Summary:** The Administration for Children and Families (ACF) proposes to remove the requirements issued in the final rule Designated Placement Requirements Under Titles IV-E and IV-B for LGBTQI+ Children (89 FR 34818) that was published on April 30, 2024. - **Link:** [https://www.federalregister.gov/documents/2026/03/06/2026-04515/designated-placement-requirements-under-titles-iv-e-and-iv-b-for-lgbtqi-children-rescission](https://www.federalregister.gov/documents/2026/03/06/2026-04515/designated-placement-requirements-under-titles-iv-e-and-iv-b-for-lgbtqi-children-rescission)

### Magnuson-Stevens Act Provisions; Fisheries Off West Coast States; Pacific Coast Groundfish Fishery; 2026 Pacific Whiting Harvest Specifications, 2026 Tribal Allocation, and 2026 Incidental Set-Aside **[NEW]**

- **Score:** 0.31
- **Source:** Federal Register
- **Published:** 2026-03-06
- **Type:** Proposed Rule
- **Summary:** NMFS issues this proposed rule to establish the 2026 Tribal allocation of Pacific whiting and set-aside of Pacific whiting for research and incidental mortality in non-groundfish fisheries. The proposed measures are intended to help prevent overfishing, achieve optimum yield, ensure that management measures are based on the best scientific information available, and provide for the implementation of Tribal treaty fishing rights.
- **Link:** [https://www.federalregister.gov/documents/2026/03/06/2026-04472/magnuson-stevens-act-provisions-fisheries-off-west-coast-states-pacific-coast-groundfish-fishery](https://www.federalregister.gov/documents/2026/03/06/2026-04472/magnuson-stevens-act-provisions-fisheries-off-west-coast-states-pacific-coast-groundfish-fishery)

### FLAGGED — Requires Specialist Attention

**FEMA BRIC** (CI: 12%)

> FEMA announced termination of BRIC on April 4, 2025. FY24/25 cycles canceled. ~$882M unobligated IIJA funds being returned to Treasury. Requires immediate specialist engagement.
>
> **Specialist Focus:** Advocacy must pivot to the replacement mechanism. Ensure Tribal set-asides are baked into the new 'responsive mitigation approach' from day one. Protect existing fully obligated projects.

## Five Structural Asks

### Enforceable consultation requirements (EO 13175) with reporting accountability [Ongoing]
**Target:** Agency
**Programs:** FEMA Tribal Mitigation Plans, BIA Tribal Climate Resilience, EPA GAP, Tribal Air Quality Management
**Mitigates:**
- Tribal Hazard Mitigation Plan required for post-disaster funding (Stafford Act § 322)

### Tribal control over climate/environmental data with federal TA funding for data infrastructure [FY26]
**Target:** Congress
**Programs:** BIA Tribal Climate Resilience, EPA GAP, EPA STAG, NOAA Tribal Grants, Tribal Air Quality Management
**Mitigates:**
- Federal data reporting requirements conflict with Tribal data sovereignty principles

### Non-competitive, formula-based, or direct-service pathways that bypass state pass-through bottlenecks [FY26]
**Target:** Agency
**Programs:** BIA Tribal Climate Resilience, DOT PROTECT, USBR WaterSMART, EPA STAG
**Mitigates:**
- Tribal set-aside administrative capacity at agencies insufficient for demand
- Competitive-only programs disadvantage Tribes without grant-writing capacity

### Categorical Tribal exemptions from cost-share requirements based on trust responsibility [Immediate]
**Target:** Congress
**Programs:** USDA Wildfire Defense Grants, USBR WaterSMART, FEMA Tribal Mitigation Plans, FEMA BRIC
**Mitigates:**
- Cost-share/match requirements across multiple programs disproportionately burden Tribes
- Cost share / match requirements (25-50%) create barriers for under-resourced Tribes

### Shift from annual discretionary to multi-year or permanent authorization for core Tribal climate programs [FY26]
**Target:** Congress
**Programs:** BIA Tribal Climate Resilience, EPA GAP, DOE Indian Energy, NOAA Tribal Grants, EPA STAG, DOT PROTECT, USBR WaterSMART, USDA Wildfire Defense Grants, HUD IHBG
**Mitigates:**
- Annual appropriations volatility creates year-to-year uncertainty for Tribal programs
- IIJA supplemental funding expires end of FY26; no reauthorization bill yet introduced
- NAHASDA reauthorization uncertainty creates long-term program risk

## Barriers & Mitigation Pathways

**Tribal set-aside administrative capacity at agencies insufficient for demand** [Med]
- Affects: DOT PROTECT, EPA STAG
- Mitigation: Non-competitive, formula-based, or direct-service pathways that bypass state pass-through bottlenecks

**Annual appropriations volatility creates year-to-year uncertainty for Tribal programs** [High]
- Affects: BIA Tribal Climate Resilience, DOE Indian Energy, NOAA Tribal Grants, EPA GAP
- Mitigation: Protect/expand the line; waived match, multi-year stability; Shift from annual discretionary to multi-year or permanent authorization for core Tribal climate programs

**BRIC program terminated April 2025; no FY24/25 cycle; ~$882M unobligated IIJA funds returned to Treasury** [High]
- Affects: FEMA BRIC
- Mitigation: Restore/replace with Tribal set-aside

**Competitive-only programs disadvantage Tribes without grant-writing capacity** [Med]
- Affects: DOT PROTECT, USBR WaterSMART
- Mitigation: Link TA to pre-award support; Non-competitive, formula-based, or direct-service pathways that bypass state pass-through bottlenecks

**Cost share / match requirements (25-50%) create barriers for under-resourced Tribes** [High]
- Affects: FEMA BRIC, USDA Wildfire Defense Grants, USBR WaterSMART
- Mitigation: Add match waivers; long-term fuels pathways; Expand Tribal drought access; lower match; Categorical Tribal exemptions from cost-share requirements based on trust responsibility

**Federal data reporting requirements conflict with Tribal data sovereignty principles** [Med]
- Affects: BIA Tribal Climate Resilience, EPA GAP, EPA STAG, NOAA Tribal Grants
- Mitigation: Tribal control over climate/environmental data with federal TA funding for data infrastructure

**Executive Order 14225 forest management reforms may restructure CWDG eligibility** [Med]
- Affects: USDA Wildfire Defense Grants
- Mitigation: Add match waivers; long-term fuels pathways

**IIJA supplemental funding expires end of FY26; no reauthorization bill yet introduced** [High]
- Affects: EPA STAG, DOT PROTECT, USBR WaterSMART, USDA Wildfire Defense Grants
- Mitigation: Shift from annual discretionary to multi-year or permanent authorization for core Tribal climate programs

**information collection request** [Med]
- Affects: DOT PROTECT

**Tribal instrumentality definition for elective pay requires legal clarity** [Med]
- Affects: IRS Elective Pay
- Mitigation: Defend against reconciliation repeal; protect Tribal instrumentality access

**Cost-share/match requirements across multiple programs disproportionately burden Tribes** [High]
- Affects: USDA Wildfire Defense Grants, USBR WaterSMART, FEMA Tribal Mitigation Plans
- Mitigation: Categorical Tribal exemptions from cost-share requirements based on trust responsibility

**Paperwork Reduction Act** [Med]
- Affects: DOT PROTECT

**Tribal Hazard Mitigation Plan required for post-disaster funding (Stafford Act § 322)** [Med]
- Affects: FEMA Tribal Mitigation Plans, FEMA BRIC
- Mitigation: Fund planning; accelerate approvals; Enforceable consultation requirements (EO 13175) with reporting accountability

**NAHASDA reauthorization uncertainty creates long-term program risk** [Med]
- Affects: HUD IHBG
- Mitigation: Shift from annual discretionary to multi-year or permanent authorization for core Tribal climate programs

**IRA provisions (§6417 Elective Pay) subject to budget reconciliation repeal** [High]
- Affects: IRS Elective Pay
- Mitigation: Defend against reconciliation repeal; protect Tribal instrumentality access
